title: Crypto Slides Monday as Middle East Tensions and Profit-Taking Weigh on Markets
dek: Crypto's weekend gains reversed into a Monday selloff as Middle East tensions resurfaced. South Korea's Kospi fell 9.2% and $253 million in leveraged crypto positions were liquidated.
author: Crypto Cronkite
key_fact: $253 million in leveraged crypto positions were wiped out during the selloff.
bottom_line: Renewed geopolitical tension and a large leverage flush combined to drive the day's move across crypto and traditional markets. What to watch is whether the $253 million in liquidations marks a one-day reset or the start of a longer stretch of risk-off trading.
body: Crypto's weekend gains gave way to a Monday selloff as Middle East tensions resurfaced, according to reporting from CoinDesk. The move followed a bullish week and coincided with broad pressure on global risk assets.
South Korea's Kospi lost 9.2% during the session, per the same reporting. In crypto markets, $253 million in leveraged positions were wiped out as prices fell.
CoinDesk reported bitcoin slipping below $63,000 during the Asian session, part of a leverage flush tied to the day's risk-off conditions.
